duration: 1:14 first time, <1:42 second time (not exactly sure)
description: Small duck (slightly smaller than nearby Blue-winged Teal) with striking white eyebrow, white stripes on wings that contrast with underparts, strong contrast between dark brown head and breast and light gray belly. Typical dabbling duck feeding behavior, and spent most of its time activey feeding. Occasionaly preened or rested, but mostly seen actively feeding. Observed flying twice, but only a short distance (~10-20 m). Stayed along the same <100m of shoreline all day, with a flock of Blue-winged and Green-winged Teal, Northern Shovelers, dowitchers, and a few other species of ducks, shorebirds and waders.
photographer: Photographer: Bryan Tarbox, Bryan Tarbox, Bryan Tarbox, Bryan Tarbox
